results.regs_verbatim:
Because regional quotas and gear restrictions are different in lakes and streams, please note: the channel between Lillooet Lake and
Little Lillooet Lake is part of the Lillooet River. This channel starts at the southern end of Lillooet Lake where it is defined by the point of
land on the west side and by a sign immediately across the channel and next to the main logging road on the east. Little Lillooet Lake's
lower boundary is approximately 1 km upstream of Billygoat Creek (see map below and Little Lillooet Lake's entry below)

land on the west side and by a sign immediately across the channel and next to the main logging road on the east. Little Lillooet Lake's
lower boundary is approximately 1 km upstream of Billygoat Creek (see map below and Little Lillooet Lake's entry below)

results.regs_verbatim:
Exempt from spring closure upstream of Thompson River, except as noted below
**No Fishing **for trout in Fraser River mainstem: downstream of Hell's Gate Sept 1-Nov 15, from Hell's Gate to the confluence with the
Thompson River from Oct 1-Nov 30, and upstream of the confluence with the Thompson River Oct 1-Oct 31
**No Fishing** between fishing boundary signs approximately 6.5 km downstream of Boston Bar to signs 2.8 km downstream of
Hell's Gate
**No Fishing** from the lower entrance of the fish ladder at Bridge River Rapids to the BC Railway Bridge approximately 2 km north of
Lillooet, Mar 31-June 20
From Hwy 99 bridge at Lillooet downstream approximately 1.2 km to BC Hydro's tail race outflow channel: **No Fishing** Oct 1-May 31;
From the confluence with Thompson River to the CNR Bridge approximately 1 km downstream: **No Fishing**, Oct 1-May 31; bait ban and no
hooks greater than 15 mm from point to shank, Oct 1-Dec 31
**No Fishing** for steelhead from the confluence with Spuzzum Creek (Region 3 boundary) to Hells Gate Aug 15-Dec 31
**No Fishing** for steelhead from Hells Gate upstream to the Region 3 boundary Jan 1-Dec 31

**There are two separate Class II waters on the Skeena River** (non-residents and non-resident aliens require separate licences describing
which section they are fishing): **(a)** from Exchamsiks River to 1.5 km upstream of Kitsumkalum River (known as "Skeena River 2"), July 1-Sept 30; and **(b)** 1.5 km upstream of Zymoetz River (known as "Skeena River Section 4"), July 1-Dec 31; **Steelhead Stamp** not mandatory for
Skeena River 2 unless fishing for steelhead. **Steelhead Stamp** is mandatory in Skeena River Section 4 during the Classified Waters period
from July 1-Dec 31
**Angling prohibited for non-guided non-resident aliens** in Skeena River Section 4 on Saturdays and Sundays in two zones: **(a)** Shegunia
River confluence to Sedan Creek confluence July 1-Dec 31 (See map page 54), and **(b)** Chimdemash Creek confluence to 1.5 km upstream of
Zymoetz River confluence, July 1 - Dec 31, (See map page 55)
